title: Tags, Versions, Releases, and Provenance · Recovery Case: Release Reproducibility
summary: Prepare a release candidate whose source identity, version, evidence, and published reference can be independently verified. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git release reproducibility increment in a signed-off release evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record clean tree, exact commit, dependency state, build/test evidence, artifacts, and checksums for a release.
Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Release Reproducibility
steps.instruction:
theory case — Release v2.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Release Reproducibility contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Bound Release Reproducibility

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record clean tree, exact commit, dependency state, build/test evidence, artifacts, and checksums for a release.

content.text: Record clean tree, exact commit, dependency state, build/test evidence, artifacts, and checksums for a release. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Release Reproducibility while producing a signed-off release evidence record.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Release v2.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. Competency probe: Record clean tree, exact commit, dependency state, build/test evidence, artifacts, and checksums for a release. A defensible solution applies Release Reproducibility, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a signed-off release evidence record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-release-reproducibility — Record clean tree, exact commit, dependency state, build/test evidence, artifacts, and checksums for a release.
git status --porcelain=v1
git show --no-patch --format=fuller HEAD
git tag -a "v5.1.0" -m 'Verified release candidate' HEAD
git show --no-patch "v5.1.0^{commit}"
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A tag alone makes a build reproducible. This fails the Release Reproducibility contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
